entry.title: Cannot create WT with Sample Split /SCWM/QINSP253
entry.content:
Hi,

we are working in an ERP BS7 connected to a decentral EWM on S/4 HANA.

We are currently upgrading the EWM to S/4 HANA 2023 FPS01.

Since the upgrade, the QM Sample Process does not work anymore, as we are unable to create putaway warehouse tasks with the following message: /SCWM/QINSP253 "Can't create warehouse tasks until external sample is calculated".

The process did work in the old release without any issues ( on S4HANA 2021 02 (05/2022) FPS)

The strange part: 

In my POV, the Inspection document is updated with the correct External Sample size quantity. The status is fine as well. 

I am unsure, why we are unable to create an inbound warehouse task.

Am I missing something here? 

Config:

EWM

Thank yo

BR

Timo

entry.title: preventive maintenance plan hierarchical strategies
entry.content:
Hello,

Does the preventive maintenance plan strategy hierarchy works same way in both scenarios:

Scenario 1: Strategy plan has only one item and in task list there is used multiple maintenance packages (example: small inspection every 1YEAR  and big inspection every 5 YEAR)

Scenario 2: Strategy plan has two maintenance items

                                    ITEM 1: small inspection  every 1 YEAR (own task list with 1 year maintenance packet)

                                    ITEM 2: big inspection every 5 YEAR (own task list with 5 year maintenance packet)

Question: Does the higher hierarchy prevent maintenance plan creation (for 1 year) in scenario 2

entry.title: Re: Document Date (TaxDate) Should not update by user in...
entry.content:
Hi,

Up until SBO FP 2405, I hadn’t encountered an SAP setting that could accomplish this directly.

This functionality can only be implemented through either a Transaction Notification or an SDK Add-on.

With a Transaction Notification, the process is straightforward: you simply compare the current value in the database column with the corresponding value in the history table, and if they differ, you prevent the update.

In the case of an SDK Add-on, it's not possible to disable system controls in update mode.

Therefore, you also need to block the click, keypress, and menu (Ctrl+V) events to ensure the functionality works as expected.

Thanks.

Manish

entry.title: Re: Purchase Order Process Control
entry.content:
HI @Jack-pyo 

Could you help me find where to configure the PO item category in the backend? Do you have the configuration path?

Also, is it possible to maintain other fields or information that could automatically unselected the Invoice Receipt? Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Enhancing Project Outcomes with SAP: Best Practices from a Project Manager's Perspective
entry.content:
@Keron .In my experience leading cross-functional SAP projects, team dynamics can present unique challenges, especially when balancing diverse skill sets and experience levels. One common issue I’ve encountered is differing expectations on timelines. While the IT side might push for quick deployment, other departments, like finance or operations, may need more time to adjust, often because they have additional compliance or data requirements to consider.

Resistance can also arise from those accustomed to legacy systems, as you mentioned. Here, fostering open communication is key. We found regular workshops and aligning on a shared vision help build buy-in, allowing the team to gradually embrace new processes. Additionally, when budget constraints come up, it’s essential to prioritize features that bring immediate value, which keeps the team focused and motivated.

entry.title: Re: Creating localized attribute by a custom type
entry.content:
What you've created is not a "localized" type. Localized types are maptypes which use Language as key (argument) type. Just adding "localized:" prefix to a maptype does not make it a localized type. A type can be a localized type even if it does not have "localized:" prefix. See for the following maptypes defined in solrfacetsearch-items.xml

SolrSynonymLangMapping
SolrKeywordRedirectLangMapping
SolrStopWordLangMapping

See following references for more information about localized attributes

  • Localized Attributes (Internationalization and Localization) 
  • Localized Attributes (Languages and Localization) 

You can see Language type control of key type of the map in various code under platform. One example is checkLocalizedChange of AttributeDescriptor in package de.hybris.platform.jalo.type

protected void checkLocalizedChange(boolean newLoc) throws JaloInvalidParameterException {
	boolean typeProvidesLocalization = false;
	Type valueType = this.getRealAttributeType();
	if (valueType instanceof MapType) {
		Type argType = ((MapType)valueType).getArgumentType();
		if (argType instanceof ComposedType && Language.class.isAssignableFrom(((ComposedType)argType).getJaloClass())) {
			typeProvidesLocalization = true;
		}
	}

	if (newLoc && !typeProvidesLocalization) {
		throw new JaloInvalidParameterException("cannot set attribute " + this + " localized since value type doesnt allow", 0);
	}
}

Unfortunately, there seems to be a no way to define which attribute you will use to select the key or value in a map. Since most of the argument or return types are atomic types (e.g. String, Integer) they can be easily imported via ImpEx like the following

INSERT_UPDATE SolrIndexedProperty; name[unique = true];valueProviderParameters[map-delimiter = |] 
;indexedPropertyName;key1->value1|key2->value2|key3->value3|key4->value4

When an itemtype is used, you can use PK of the item when importing, but it cannot be done in single-run since PK value could not be available before the item is created. So I think your best choice is to use a Translator. You can find several Translators to investigate how you can write a new one. Also read more from here in Translator documentation

entry.title: SAP GTS - Import Declaration after goods receipt - no items available in worklist.
entry.content:
Hi everybody,

we are facing a Problem with respect to Import Declaration after goods receipt.

Our System:
- S/4 HANA 2023 FPS01
- GTS 11, SP 24

We intend to use the "Import declaration after goods receipt"-Scenario (Germany / European Union) following a completed Transit Process.

Inbound deliveries are created and transfered correctly to GTS (when T1 and MRN are inserted), and also the presentation is created correctly. But when we then do Goods Receipt posting via MIGO (referencing the inbound delivery), no Inbound activities are listed (more specifically, no data are entered in /SAPSLL/CUSWLO ==> we get a “no data found” information message). Apparently, the goods receipt is transferred successfully to GTS (e.g. for risk management). We noticed, that in /SAPSLL/CUSWLM field DLDNO is empty. If anybody could help us with some ideas that'd be great.

Thanks, Manuel

entry.title: Re: 400 Bad HTTP Request
entry.content: Standard HTTP status codes are between 100 and 599. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_HTTP_status_codes. I'm surprised that a non-standard code is rejected by the kernel. Use SICF menu > client > recorder to get the exact request and response. After that, you may also contact the SAP Support (I didn't find an SAP note about this message, but maybe https://me.sap.com/notes/2620715/E can give clues about the problem). NB: with the help of a developer, I would create my own web service on the same server, which returns 610, to test whether the kernel accepts a non-standard HTTP status code, that will prove that the problem is not directly due to 610.

entry.title: Re: How can I see the previous (moved developments) devel...
entry.content: @yalcin_mete Look at the import queue history to see which transport requests were imported (STMS).

entry.title: Flexible Column Layout, havigate directly to Object Page in full screen mode
entry.content:
Hello.

I'm developing Fiori Elements application, that uses Flexible Column Layout. The app starts with list report and from it navigates to Object Page in TwoColumnsMidExpanded mode. So far so good.

But now I have also external navigation to my app, that opens directly Object Page for specified entity. And in that case, it always opens in TwoColumnsMidExpanded mode having empty (not loaded) list report on the left pane.

Is there any possibility to open Object Page in full screen mode (MidColumnFullScreen) when coming from external navigation? I tried defining different routes/targets, but nothing works. Actually, when I add any new route or target referring somehow to my Object Page, it breaks the main app processing.

I'm referring to similar old thread there:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/flexible-column-layout-hide-begin-column-list-report/qaq-p/700439

entry.title: Recheck Documented Credit Decision automatically?
entry.content:
Hello,

Is there a program we can run in the background that would "recheck" documented credit decision cases?

Let's say a sales order document is on credit block because the customer's credit limit is exceeded. After the customer made a payment, the blocked document should get rechecked because the credit exposure has been reduced enough to allow the sales order to get created, thus allowing the sales order to be released.

Would program RVKRED09 serve this function?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Recheck Documented Credit Decision automatically?
entry.content:
Hello,
 
Yes, there are background reports to trigger new credit check for existing sales documents when the credit situation changes (e.g. increasing credit limit, getting payment, etc.).
 
RVKRED06 - The report checks all blocked documents from a credit point of view. It should be started in the background after incoming payments
RVKRED08 - The report rechecks all sales documents which occur in the horizon of the dynamic credit check. The report should be started for each period at the start of the period.
RVKRED09 - The report RVKRED09 is designed for checking the credit documents from credit view in background. The report performs new credit checks, and gives you a range of selection options.
 
In general I would advise RVKRED09 as it gives most flexibility with the selection options.
 
Best regards,
Edina
SAP

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Maintenance Windows and Major Upgrade Windows in 2025
entry.content:
One of the benefits of using SAP BTP ABAP Environment is that updates and upgrades are applied by SAP to the systems in quite a high frequency and close to the corresponding release time.
Find the latest known schedule of Major Upgrade Windows and Weekly Maintenance Windows for 2025 in this blog post.

 

Major Upgrade Windows

As in 2024, the SAP BTP ABAP Environment will have 4 Major Upgrade Windows in 2025. These 24 hours of potential system unavailability are used to upgrade the systems to the next major version.
The events for the Major Upgrade Windows are harmonized between all SAP Cloud Products and scheduled for the following weekends:

  • February 15th – 16th (Release 2502) 
  • May 17th – 18th (Release 2505) 
  • August 16th – 17th (Release 2508) 
  • November 15th – 16th (Release 2511) 

Depending on the data center region, the 24 hours downtime will start/end at:

  • APJ: FRI 9 pm – SAT 9 pm UTC
  • Europe: SAT 4 am – SUN 4 am UTC
  • Americas: SAT 10 am – SUN 10 am UTC

The upgrade of the free tier systems takes place on the Thursday before the respective weekend.

 

Weekly Maintenance Windows

Potentially every weekend, the SAP BTP ABAP Environment could use a downtime of 4 hours, to apply database updates, Hotfix Collections and other changes.
Depending on the data center region, the 4 hours downtime may start at:

  • APJ: SAT 3 pm UTC
  • Europe: SAT 10 pm UTC
  • Americas: SUN 4 am UTC

The update of the free tier systems takes place on the Thursday before the respective weekend.
The following events for the release 2502 are planned as Weekly Maintenance Windows in 2025:

  • Hotfix Collection 4 for Release 2502 (only for systems which belong to the pre-upgrade)
    February 15th – 16th, 2025
  • Hotfix Collection 5 for Release 2502 | February 26th – 27th, 2025
  • Hotfix Collection 6 for Release 2502 | March 15th – 16th, 2025
  • Hotfix collection 7 for Release 2502 | March 29th - 30th, 2025

For more details about the Reduction of Planned Maintenance Windows for HFC Imports and Upgrades see our roadmap.


Client Safety of CDS and AMDP

To support client-safe implementation please read Client Handling in ABAP - SAP Community for an overview.
Technical background is provided in SAP Note 3472859 - AMDP: How to make AMDP methods client-safe.

Please adopt these recommendations. SAP will turn today's warning messages into error messages with the upgrade to release 2508, which is planned for August 2025.

 

Cloud System Notification Subscriptions (CSNS)

Please register to Cloud System Notification Subscriptions (CSNS) to get important information about planned and unplanned downtimes:

  • Access to Cloud System Notification Subscriptions (CSNS): https://me.sap.com/systemsprovisioning/getNotified
  • Find more information about Cloud System Notification Subscriptions (CSNS) in this documentation and video guide
  • To find the SAP BTP ABAP Environment entry select:
    Cloud Product: SAP Extension Suite - Development Efficiency
    Cloud Service: SAP BTP ABAP environment


References

  • Maintenance Windows and Major Upgrade Windows for SAP Cloud Services
  • SAP Note 2888562 – Intelligent Enterprise Suite: Harmonized release calendar for SAP Cloud products
  • Service Level Agreement for SAP Cloud Services

entry.title: we require non expiry sap user ID for the interface with other software's
entry.content:
Hi,


we are using the SAP user id to post the interface data in to the sap and we maintained the sap user id and password in the all interface API and CPI to post the data in sap s4 PERSON_NAME. but every 6 months sap user id password is expiring due to that interfaces are failing.


we required non expiry password for SAP user id to use in the interface.


 


Regards,


Manjunatha Reddy.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: we require non expiry sap user ID for the interface w...
entry.content:
Hello,
its easy to achieve that by selecting the correct user-Type for your requirement.

Please read the documentation in SU01 regarding the user-types on the Logon data tab. It says:

quote
User TypeDialog 'A'

A normal dialog user is used by one person only for all types of logon.

During a dialog logon, the system checks for expired and initial passwords and gives you the option to change your password.

Multiple dialog logons are checked and, where appropriate, logged.

System 'B'

Use the system user type for internal system processes (-> background processing) or system-related processes (-> ALE, workflow, TMS, CUA).

Dialog logon (using SAP GUI) is not possible.

A user of this type is excluded from the general settings for password validity. Only user administrators can change the password using transaction SU01 (Goto -> Change Password).

Multiple logons are allowed.

Communication 'C'

Use users of type Communication for dialog-free communication between systems (-> RFC or CPIC) .

Dialog logon (using SAP GUI) is not possible.

The general settings for the validity period of a password apply to users of this type. Users of this type can change their passwords (like dialog users). The dialogs for changing the password must be provided by the caller (RFC/CPIC client). You can use the RFC function module SUSR_USER_CHANGE_PASSWORD_RFC or the RFC API function RfcOpenEx() to change the password.

Service 'S'

A user of the type Service is a dialog user that is available to an anonymous, larger group of users. Generally, this type of user should only be assigned very restricted authorizations.

For example, service users are used for anonymous system access using an ITS service or a public Web service. Once an individual has been authenticated, a session that started anonymously using a service user can be continued as a personal session using a dialog user (see SUSR_INTERNET_USERSWITCH)

During logon, the system does not check for expired and initial passwords. Only the user administrator can change the password.

Multiple logons are allowed.

Reference 'L'

Like the service user, a reference user is a general user, not assigned to a particular person. You cannot log on using a reference user. The reference user is only used to assign additional authorizations. Reference users are implemented to equip Internet users with identical authorizations.

On the Roles tab, you can specify a reference user for additional rights for dialog users. In general, the application controls the assignment of reference users. The name of the reference user can be assigned using variables. The variables should begin with "$". You assign variables to reference users in transaction SU_REFUSERVARIABLE.

This assignment applies to all systems in a CUA landscape. If the assigned reference user does not exist in one of the CUA child systems, the assignment is ignored.

Overview

Overview of the properties of users of the different user types:

Property / User Type                       Dialog          Communication             System              Service

Dialog logon (SAP GUI)                   X                 -                                       -                  X

Password Change                           X                X                                        -                   -

Logon ticket can be generated          X                X                                         -                 -

 

Apart from reference users, users of all types can log on to a system using RFC or execute background jobs.

unquote

So for your requirement user type 'System' seems to be appropriate. Please discuss with your user administrator.

 

b.rgds,
Bernhard

entry.title: Exception Handling in Workflow for Sales Documents
entry.content:
During the creation of a workflow in the "Workflow for Sales Documents" app, in the exception handling part for the Rework Requested only "Restart Workflow" option is available as an Action Result. 

In SWDD_SCENARIO, for the sales order approval WS02000006 in the rework of sales order step all of the action results seems to be available, but we can't choose other options in the workflow app.

Does anyone know the solution for this issue, our customer want to use other options such as Continue in case of a Rework. 

Thank you,

entry.title: My customer is moving from ECC to RISE, MPLS issue
entry.content: My customer is moving from ECC to RISE and we are having major delay (2 months) in the MPLS connectivity between ISP provider and C4C. Is there any resource or team that can help us move forward with the project?

entry.title: Re: How can I see the previous (moved developments) devel...
entry.content: @yalcin_mete Transaction SE03 (transport tools) to find the transport requests which contain one given object. Also, in your development system, go to the object and display the versions of the object to see all its transport requests (unless you have emptied the version database at some point in time).

entry.title: Re: Public Cloud: EDI Configuration for Update/Cancel Pur...
entry.content:
Hi,

Please maintain Fields Relevant to Printouts of Change as shown in the SAP Blog:  https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/how-to-generate-purchase-order-output-on-change-of-a-purchase-order/ba-p/13689606.

When there is a change in the value of the field which are marked as print relevant, a new output should be issued. 

Best regards,

Meghana

entry.title: What's new in DTV 1.5
entry.content:
It’s a been a while since DTV 1.5 is released, and I am more than happy to share more information regarding the latest and greatest release of Data Transition Validation Tool.

Partial Execution

In DTV 1.5 partial execution of data simulation, extraction and evaluation is now enabled in work item level. In a real time, scenario, when we define multiple split parameters for greater granularity of data, execution of huge amount of data may lead to failures sometimes. With partial execution enablement, now users can execute simulation, extraction, and evaluation of individual failed work items instead of the whole test specification saving valuable time during business data evaluation in business down time.

Specify Maximum number of work processes

Whether it is Simulation, Extraction or Evaluation, DTV creates background jobs for processing and executing work items. For optimum system performance, now you can define dedicated number of work processes which can be utilized for DTV for background jobs, further finetuning the DTV overall performance.

You can do the setup in Maintain Systems -> Max. num of Processes.

Result Data

The entire result data of the reports including the hidden fields can now be seen in the Result Data making the analysis easy and intuitive for the end user.

Copy the project including the Test Specification

In DTV 1.5 you have the possibility to copy the project global data and the test specifications to further improve the usability of the tool.

 

Execution Runtime information is enhanced with delay time calculations

entry.title: From where in S4 can I find the SAC values for Rate types like average and closing (currency)?
entry.content:
I'm currently building a model in SAC where data is imported from S4. The dimensions used in the model are Profit Center, Cost Center, GL Account, and Company Code. 

As I need currency conversion in the model, I am wondering if I can obtain directly from the system the SAC type required for the currency conversion such as average or closing. 

For example I know that for GL Account, there is a specific property called SAC Account Type available in an API in S4 so that you can upload the data in SAC with the correct SAC type. Im wondering if there is something similar in terms of currency. 

Thanks,

entry.title: SAP Build process automation - Document Template - Error
entry.content:
Hi,

We built a process using SAP Document Information Extraction Using Templates, and it worked fine.

Now we created new subaccount and we move our project there, but we are getting an error when we try to create/change/use a template.

The message is "SAP_IPA_28014: The requested Document Information Extraction service operation could not be completed. Call to Document Information Extraction failed with status 400 BAD_REQUEST.

I checked and I have the role collection ProcessAutomationDeveloper that contains the role Document_Information_Extraction_UI_Templates_Admin.

What else can be missing?

Thanks

SAP Build Process Automation , Document Information Extraction

entry.title: Production and Maintenance Order Settlement
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a question about Production and Maintenance Order Settlement. When I settle Production order with consumes I see also quantities that are settled in accounting document, while in Maintenance order, always with consumes this does not happen.

Is there any setting that I'm missing? Or this is the standard SAP behaviour?

Thank you,

Pietro

entry.title: Re: Equipment Status to INAC
entry.content:
Hi Preethialevarthi,

Equipment-Asset synchronization (by standard) can be done only for a predefined set of fields. In the IMG route: Financial Accounting -> Asset Accounting -> Master Data -> Automatic creation of equipment master records -> Assign Master Data fields of Assets and Equipment, you can find the fields that can be mapped.

Before this you have to map asset class with equipment category and give conditions in "Specify conditions for synchronization of Master Date" node.

If you want to synchronize more fields, you have to go with "Develop Enhancement for Field and Class Assignment" IMG node (Customer Enhancement: AAPM0001). It is recommended to only create a synchronization between data fields for which the following is true:

    • The fields are defined syntactically the same. (Not same in the case of INAC eqp. status and deletion of asset)
    • The fields are not primary or foreign keys.

Refer note : 370884 - PM-AA 4.6C: Consulting on sync activation + corrections - SAP for Me

Alternatively:
You can use the BADI: EQUI_UPDATE or User Exit: IEQM0003 to detect equipment status change to INAC (Status type code = 'I0320').

For updating the asset master record, you can use the functional module ASSET_MASTERRECORD_MAINTENANCE. Field for deletion flag: I_ANLA - XSPEB

Hope this answer gave you a brief overview to help your case.

Regards,
Ritesh PR

entry.title: Re: change withholding tax base amount in S4HC
Re: Customer exit SUSR0001 to log off invalid user
entry.content:
Hi Sandra,

I removed the wait step and replaced FM TH_POPUP with POPUP_TO_INFORM. As you said, its showing the message and if the user clicks the green checkmark in the popup, he is logged off as I am calling the FM SYST_LOGOFF if sy-subrc eq 0.

But I see that the user still has the option to click the top left corner in the popup to open a new session and still login to SAP outside his shift hours as seen in the below image. 

Kindly let me know if its possible not to allow the user to take any backdoor entry like this to login to SAP.

Thanks

Bharath

entry.title: Re: change withholding tax base amount in S4HC
entry.content:
Please try follwing below steps.

In order to change Withholding Tax Code please follow the steps below:

  1. APP :Schedule Accounts Payable Jobs
  2. Click on + sign and choose the Job Template Recreate and Change Withholding Tax Data with 0% Withholding Tax Rate (RFWT0020)
  3. Input the needed parameters and press Schedule
    There is a checkbox Start Immediately - if checked, the report will be executed immediately.
    Also there is the checkbox for Test mode - so you can check the potential changes, before running the report in real mode and make changes to database entries.

entry.title: Re: ****HOW TO SHOW MONTHLY DATA IN DIFFERENT ROW WISE -...
entry.content:
3 different tables

Table 1 filtered for Jan-Apr

Table 2 filtered for May-Aug

Table 3 filtered for Sep-Dec

Use relative positioning for the 3 tables to make it look like it is one big table

regards

James

entry.title: I like, I wish
entry.content:
Dear course participants,

Your input helps us tremendously in shaping and improving our courses and the platform. This is why we ask all participants to share their thoughts after completing a course.

The “I like, I wish” method allows you to structure your feedback. If you’ve never used it before, see the following examples:

* “I like that the course content was broken down into smaller lessons”.
* “I wish I could log on to learning.sap.com with my LinkedIn credentials”.

Please try and use this approach to share your feedback with us. Although we can’t reply to these posts, we promise to read each and every suggestion and discuss them within the team.

Thank you for choosing SAP Learning!

Kind regards,
Britta Radicke-Schlinsog

(SAP Learning team)

entry.title: Re: Using Current Date Input Control in SAC Advanced Filters
entry.content: Have you tried API: Application.setCurrentDateTime();

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud: Newly Created Members Delay In S...
entry.content:
Hello - I have used the create member followed by a data action several times and have not encountered a delay. The data action is able to pick up the new member straight away within the same script without delay timers etc.

When the data action fails I've found instead it's caused by the syntax of the dimension member you are passing across. For example passing a member without a hierarchy you can just pass the ID, but if passing a member to the data action from script that is in a hierarchy, then you have to add the full technical hierarchy information around the ID like below:

var id = InputField_ID.getValue();

var id2 = "[DIMENSION].[H1].&["+id+"]"; // restate ID so that the Data Action can recognise this

..then id2 is passed to the DA

DataAction_1.setParameterValue("DIMENSION",id2);

Just want to check this is not the issue first as I've not experienced any delays before upon master data creation.

David

entry.title: Re: Report to update PGI status in SAP EWM
entry.content: If the goods issue status (DGI) on the outbound delivery (document category FDO) was set correctly in EWM, a typical root cause for the status 'Completion' (DCO) to be partially completed is that the status 'Transit Status' (DOY) was not set.

I am using basic credential of my service instance.

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Dear Jayshree,

Relating to your ask about MRP integration and the document type needed for the Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer (5HP) here the standard view is as follows.

The process of using MRP to generate Purchase Requisitions can be used also in conjunction with 5HP and specifically herein the special document type needed for the Stock Transfer Order (standard delivered one being NBIC).

In General it bears worth noting, that the Purchase Requisition Item generated from MRP for stock transfer cases will always be categorized to be of type "Stock Transfer" (named as "U" usually, internally referenced as item category 7). 

Thus, regardless of the new scenario 5HP, there is a difference between the Purchase Requisition Item Category and the Purchase Order Item Category in case of intercompany stock transfers.

So the topic is not at all new in any sense. 

What needs to be done is to tell the system, that there needs to be considered a conversion of item category during the PR-PO conversion (again, true for both "existing" intercompany stock transfer as well as for the new advanced intercompany stock transfer). 

This can be achieved via customizing of the Purchase Order Document Type NBIC. To be more precise, you can here from the document type "NBIC" select the allowed item category blank. There in turn you can go one step further, to link purchase requisitions. There you can reference the PR document type "NB" and PR item category "U". Thereby a conversion can consider that PR items of type NB/U are convertible into PO items of type "NBIC/blank".

Kind regards

Thomas Jürgensen

entry.title: Re: SAP UI5 PDF View doesn't work any more and have to do...
entry.content:
You need to set the parameter "isTrustedSource" to true.

In version 1.71.63 a new property "isTrustedSource" was introduced.:

[FIX] We've introduced the isTrustedSource property as a security enhancement for the PDFViewer control as detailed in the API reference

see API: https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/1.71.70/#/api/sap.m.PDFViewer/controlProperties

Parameter to determine if the given PDF is from a trusted source. If the source is valid this property can be set to true. If isTrustedSource is set to true, the PDFViewer opens with the displayType set to "Embedded" on desktop devices. This means that the PDF content is directly shown within the PDFViewer. Set this property to true only when the PDF is generated by the application or the PDF is scanned for viruses. If isTrustedSource is set to false, the PDFViewer opens with the displayType set to "Link" on desktop devices. This means that any configuration that has been provided by the application for the property displayType is overridden. In this case, the user would need to download the PDF to view its content.

entry.title: Re: Mashup from SAP Build Not Loading in SAP С4С Mobile App

entry.title: What is the added value to hide email & phone number in New People Profile > Spotlight ?
entry.content:
in the new people profile, the spotlight view is no longer displaying email address & phone number.

I would like to know what was the rational on sap side to do so ? 

on pc, these is causing the default mail program to be opened when clicking on email.

on mobile, it's the same, it's opening the default email program.

it's no longer easy to copy / paste email address & phone number from spotlight.

so i would like to understand what lead for such dramatic change.

entry.title: #06 - Utilize SAP Best Practices: Maximize Your Project Success with SAP Signavio Process Navigator
entry.content:
Implementing an SAP solution is a significant investment and ensuring that you leverage the full potential of SAP's tools and methodologies is crucial to achieving success. One of the powerful resources at your disposal is the SAP Signavio Process Navigator* which will give you access to SAP Best Practices.

*an S-User ID is required to access the SAP Signavio Process Navigator.

Why should you utilize SAP Best Practices?

Leveraging SAP Best Practices offers numerous advantages for your project:

  • Accelerated Implementation: SAP Best Practices are designed to speed up the implementation process by providing pre-configured solutions based on proven methodologies.

  • Reduced Risk: By following established best practices, you minimize the risk of errors and ensure that your implementation is aligned with industry standards.

  • Cost Efficiency: Implementing SAP Best Practices reduces the need for extensive customizations, saving time and resources.

  • Continuous Improvement: SAP continuously updates its best practices based on real-world feedback and new developments, ensuring that you always have access to the latest and most effective practices.

What is the SAP Signavio Process Navigator?

The SAP Signavio Process Navigator is a cloud service integrated in SAP for Me* which provides insights (at no additional cost) to Customers and Partners about SAP’s Best Practices process portfolio following a business and solution process-centric data model and design.

It delivers process models, process documentation, process related value accelerators, country and industry specific implementation guidance to help businesses “run simple”, for most of SAP’s solutions, in all deployment types, public and private cloud, on-premise or hybrid scenarios.

*an S-User ID is required to access SAP for Me.

Key Features of the SAP Signavio Process Navigator

  • Access to SAP Best Practices: it provides access to a vast library of SAP Best Practices, which are pre-configured, ready-to-deploy business processes that align with SAP's vast experience and industry standards.

  • Detailed Documentation: it offers detailed documentation, including product and process descriptions, test scripts, and configuration guides. This documentation helps you understand how to implement SAP solutions efficiently.

  • Process Diagrams: visualize your business processes with detailed process diagrams, making it easier to see how different components fit together.

Figure 1: How SAP Signavio Process Navigator supports an implementation project.

Solutions Covered

SAP Signavio Process Navigator covers a wide range of SAP solutions, ensuring that no matter which SAP product you are implementing, you will find relevant best practices and documentation. Some of the solutions covered include:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP Integrated Business Planning
  • SAP SuccessFactors
  • SAP Ariba
  • SAP Business Network for Supply Chain
  • SAP Fieldglass

Each solution has its own set of best practices, tailored to optimize the implementation and operation of that product.

Recommendations

To fully leverage the power of SAP Signavio Process Navigator, follow these recommendations:

  1. Check the Getting Started Guide: learn everything that you need to know about SAP Signavio Process Navigator and where to start.

  2. Stay Updated: regularly check the Info Corner and the News Center for the latest updates and hotfixes to ensure that you’re working with the most current information.

  3. Engage with the SAP Community: join the SAP Community to share experiences, ask questions, and learn from other users who are also utilizing SAP Best Practices and SAP Signavio Process Navigator.

  4. Provide Feedback: use the feedback feature on SAP Signavio Process Navigator to let SAP know about your experience with the platform. This helps SAP to continually improve the resources available to you.

Conclusion

Utilizing SAP Best Practices is a critical component of a successful SAP implementation. With the SAP Signavio Process Navigator, you have access to a wealth of knowledge and resources that can significantly streamline your project, reduce risks, and ensure that you achieve the best possible outcomes. By taking full advantage of the tools and methodologies available through the SAP Signavio Process Navigator, you set your project on the path to success, delivering value to your organization quickly and efficiently.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Check out other blogs from the series   Customer Onboarding at SAP
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

About the author:

  • Inês Moura supports with the design and orchestration of the customer onboarding journey for SAP RISE customers, ensuring a seamless experience from signature to successful go-live. As an Onboarding Advisor, Inês provides expert guidance and enablement, facilitating a smooth transition from the sales cycle to project start. With a focus on targeted project guidance and risk mitigation, Inês is dedicated to supporting SAP customers throughout the entire onboarding journey.

Having questions, comments or feedbacks? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact @InesMoura or @Linh-Hoang .

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: Planning Table for 2 or more workcenters (repetitive manufacturing
entry.content:
Hi 

SAP S/4HANA Public cloud.

I have a customer who can produce the sale product on 2 or 3 production lines.

I would like to use Repetitive manufacturing, but I don't know how to show all 3 lines onthe Planning Board.

I have created different routings and versions.

Teoreticaly it shoud be easy to manupulate with line load per assignning different lines.

Is the any possibility to do so?

There is no WC Hierarchy in Public cloud.

Rgds

MAciej

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

That's it! We have reached the deadline for new applicants. I appreciate everyone who took the time to write and sign up for the digital skills initiative.

I have a huge backlog of completed learning journey requests to go through, so please bare with me as I verify each one. I will get to you within the next 2 weeks and send you your free certification attempt.

All applicants have until December 1 to submit their completed learning journey to qualify for a free certification attempt.

If you have arrived here after the deadline, see the bottom of this blog to understand how you can get started with learning SAP: Celebrating 2 Years of the Digital Skills Initiative

Thanks everyone!

entry.title: Re: Setting status of equipment / functional location and...
entry.content:
Did you alread look at the (others) BAPI/API of function groups ITOB_BAPI_EQ Equipment and ITOB_BAPI_FL Func location?

  • There are BAPI for some system statuses: e.g. BAPI_EQUI_INSTALL, IBAPI_FUNCLOC_SET_INACTIVE
  • There are some BAPI for all user statuses e.g. IBAPI_EQUI_USERSTATUS_CHANGE, IBAPI_FUNCLOC_USERSTATUS_CHANG

Else which statuses did you try to set or reset?

entry.title: Re: how to add an attribute of a different type in advanc...
entry.content: Hello @Alex,
